title: Doctors Go on Strike in England
summary: Doctors in England have started a six-day strike because they want better pay. These doctors, called resident doctors, work in hospitals and make up almost half of the medical staff. The strike began on Tuesday morning and is causing big problems for hospitals. Many appointments and treatments are being canceled. Senior doctors are helping in emergencies, but regular care is affected. The government and doctors' union talked but could not agree. Patients with emergencies should still call 999 or 111. The government says the strike costs a lot of money, but doctors say their pay has not kept up with rising prices. Both sides are sorry for the trouble but blame each other for the strike.

the money people get for doing their job

What is the main reason for the strike?

Who is helping in emergencies during the strike?
What are many appointments and treatments doing because of the strike?

background_read: In England, doctors work in the National Health Service (NHS) to help people stay healthy. Sometimes, doctors feel they are not paid enough for their hard work, so they go on strike, which means they stop working to ask for more money. This can cause problems in hospitals because there are fewer doctors to care for patients. Strikes happen when the government and doctors' union cannot agree on pay. It is important for patients to know what to do during a strike, like calling for help in emergencies.
Article_Structure: WHAT: A six-day strike over pay, causing canceled appointments
WHERE: In England's hospitals and NHS services
WHY: Because talks between the government and doctors' union broke down, with doctors wanting better pay

perspectives.description: Doctors say their pay has not kept up with rising prices, so they need a better deal to keep working in the NHS.
The government says they offered a good deal and the strike is costly, blaming doctors for not accepting it.
